parsed_review.summary: CP00-334 is a descriptor-only intake-core failure slice (RP10.P07.M03.S15-RP10.P07.M04.S02, 10 units, risk A) that honestly declares production_ready:false / runtime_ready:false. The modest descriptor-verification claim is fully supported by static inspection: registry.js/service.js/validators.js build only frozen descriptor objects with zero IO/network/DB/child_process/runtime (grep confirmed no matches) and internal-only imports. Coverage (M03:8 + M04:2), deliverable distribution, unit IDs, and next_subphase are consistent across plan, contract, registry, and manifest. All 7 required public exports exist and are exercised by tests; index.js re-exports via wildcard (explaining why it is unmodified). The no-write/no-leak boundary is enforced by validation guards backed by the inherited row-extras chain (blocked_claim_detail_included:false, real_client_data_loaded:false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, expected_outcome rejected_customer_safe, etc.). Claude is correctly kept as a read-only, non-final reviewer (claude_is_final_approval:false, human_final_approval_required:true), asserted by the validate script, and forbidden_review_evidence covers all CP178+ hardening cases. Hermes appears only as deterministic descriptor strings, not product runtime. Auth preflight passed with valid JSON and Read/Grep/Glob-only tools. Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are correctly absent at this pre-review stage. Only one cosmetic P3 metadata nit found; nothing blocks pack or goal closeout.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-lists pack evidence dir as both implementation and unrelated
findings.details: docs/closeout-packs/cp00-334/ (the pack's own evidence directory) and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appear simultaneously in untracked_implementation_files (lines 130-133) and in unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 137-138). A file cannot logically be both pack implementation and unrelated. This is a benign labeling contradiction in generated review metadata only; the authoritative active_pack_implementation_refs and active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted ([]) are correct, the manifest enumerates required_pack_artifacts explicitly, and 'preserved' merely means leave-as-is, so there is no functional impact on closeout gating, the production_ready claim, tests, or boundaries.
findings.recommendation: In the changed-file-scope generator, exclude the active pack's own evidence directory (and the pack handoff doc) from unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, or omit them from untracked_implementation_files, so each path lands in exactly one bucket. Non-blocking cleanup.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking. Cosmetic metadata inconsistency in generated review tooling; does not affect CP00-334 implementation, validation, or descriptor-only boundaries. May be addressed in a future generator tweak.
